requirements.evidence: LSCard token mapping is implemented in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSCard.swift and asserted by ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSCardTests.swift; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS with 7 total tests green.
LSPanel token mapping is implemented in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSPanel.swift and asserted by ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSPanelTests.swift; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS.

The chrome glass variant uses .background(.ultraThinMaterial) in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSGlassPanel.swift and is asserted by ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSGlassPanelTests.swift; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS.

LSGlassPanel callout(.signal) renders a 3pt leading stripe in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSGlassPanel.swift and the signal token-path contract is asserted in ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSGlassPanelTests.swift; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS.

LSGlassPanel callout(.warning) renders a 3pt leading stripe in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSGlassPanel.swift and the warning token-path contract is asserted in ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSGlassPanelTests.swift; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS.

Padding flows through the typed Spacing enum in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/AccentColor.swift and is exercised by ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSCardTests.swift for the spacing5 override; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS.

The AccentColor type-safe API is declared in ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/AccentColor.swift and ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSGlassPanelTypeSafetyTests.swift asserts raw Color is not accepted; targeted xcodebuild test reported PASS.
All seven required story ids and LSSurfaceStories aggregation are present in ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/LaneShadowStories.swift; the host grep gate reported PASS.
ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSCard.swift, LSPanel.swift, and LSGlassPanel.swift contain no literal Color usage on inspection, and the host grep gate reported PASS.

findings.evidence: AccentColor.signal advertises token path color.signal.default, but the current Swift theme surface resolves it through theme.colors.primary.default because the generated theme does not expose a dedicated signal accessor yet.
findings.fix: When the theme pipeline exposes a first-class signal color on iOS, switch AccentColor.signal to that accessor and keep the existing tests as the safety net.

summary: Approved. The surface atoms satisfy the task scope, the targeted XCTest/build/grep evidence is green, and I found no merge blocker under the sprint's relaxed review standard. The only residual issue is the existing theme-pipeline fallback for signal color resolution.
